name: Bearcatmon
background:
A Beast Man Digimon characterized by its large fists and roller skates. Although comparatively small in stature, Bearcatmon possesses extremely high physical abilities, including top-notch strength and speed among Ultimate-level Digimon. Many individual specimens have belligerent personalities and often wander the Digital World seeking opponents to fight. If one happens upon a Digimon of equal or higher ability, it will attack immediately without hesitation. However, if it considers the Digimon to be of a lower rank, it will simply leave without a word. Digimon who are abruptly assaulted find these encounters extremely wearisome, while Digimon who are not attacked feel humiliated at being considered weak. As such, few Digimon welcome an encounter with Bearcatmon.
Penetrate Blow is Bearcatmon's most powerful special move, utilizing the jet propulsion of its roller skates together with the full strength of its body to deliver a devastating knifehand strike. Its sharp bear claws have the power to literally pierce through an opponent's body. With Murderize Rush, Bearcatmon rains blows upon its opponent with hardened knuckles. The attack is executed at such high speed that it is difficult to evade by sight alone. Beardown Spinning Kick employs the jet propulsion of the roller skates to send Bearcatmon high into the air, where it spins to gather momentum and then delivers a potent dropkick. Although less powerful than Penetrate Blow, this technique's flashy appearance means it is often used to show off.

name: Taowumon
background:
A Dark Beast Digimon that resembles a boar enveloped in cold air, with tusks composed of tree trunks. It is the largest and most thick-witted of the Digimon feared as the Shikyoju.
　Taowumon rarely shows itself due to spending most of its time burrowed underground, slumbering like a solid mass of ice. Once it awakens, it shakes its tail, causing the skull on the end to emit cold air and transform the surroundings into a frigid landscape. It then charges to the farthest ends of Jakeizan, where it rams its head into the barrier that forever binds it to the mountain, over and over again.
　The seals placed on its tusks reveal traces of data belonging to one particular Digimon, said to be the god who rules over the cycle of rebirth throughout the eastern Digital World. This being is suspected to have been closely involved in the sealing away of the Shikyoju.
　Its Skull Iceshards special move has it shoot out chunks of ice from the skull on its tail, and Thunderous Upheaval sees it slam its forelegs down, creating fissures in the ground. For its Freezing Tusk special move, it performs a charging attack with its icicle-bearing tusks—a simple maneuver, but nonetheless fatal if it hits.

name: ScourgeChiropmon
background:
A Machine Digimon whose body resembles an aircraft. It is theorized that it absorbed design data for a stealth bomber when Digivolving. The dark coloration and a fuselage designed to thwart radar and other sensors make the routinely nocturnal ScourgeChiropmon extremely difficult to detect. With only three sightings on record, these Digimon are rarely encountered, but it is unclear if this is because there are so few of them or simply because their stealth capabilities make spotting them nearly impossible.
Sensor drones stored atop both winds called Clingy Bros can be deployed to pinpoint a target's coordinates with Triangulation Scan. ScourgeChiropmon can then use its most devastating offensive maneuver, Despair Blast, which launches the guided missiles suspended beneath the wings. These missiles are thermobaric explosives, meaning that a wide area around the target will also be caught in the blast. Additionally, ScourgeChiropmon can use Eliminate Rain—a carpet-bombing run with bat-shaped cluster bombs—to wipe out anything on ground level. While dishing out massive damage is this Digimon's specialty, it also wields non-lethal skills, such as using Silent Scream to emit super high frequency sound waves from its ears to stun the target.
There is a space like a cockpit in its head that can fit a Rookie-sized Digimon; however, no Digimon were observed on board in any of the past three sightings.

name: Monarchlizamon
background: A Cyborg Digimon remodeled for assault operations. The propulsion provided by the massive vernier thrusters mounted on its back allow Monarchlizamon to move around quickly despite its large size. Its left arm is equipped with Risanautr, an assault weapon that combines a double-headed axe with a plasma cannon, allowing it to unleash a wide variety of attacks consisting of thrusts, slashes, and shots. Annihilate Edge is a slashing attack that channels high-temperature plasma energy through Risanautr's blades, melting through its target and inflicting severe damage. With the great agility provided by its vernier thrusters, it can rapidly close in on its opponent, then use Monarch Ordinance to pierce its opponent with Risanautr and fire the plasma cannon at point-blank range. Against opponents of a smaller build, this attack is powerful enough to vaporize them in a single strike. The distinctive crescent-shaped horn on its forehead functions as a plasma energy generator. The plasma produced by the horn is used as fuel for the vernier thrusters and Risanautr. Its special move, Final Judgment, uses a full charge of the plasma generated by its crescent horn to create a blade of plasma to slice through its opponent. The special move involves a massive leap using its vernier thrusters, followed by a thruster-powered high-speed vertical spin in midair, and a body slam that adds to the force of its descent to hit with maximum power.

name: Apollomon:Whispered
background:
The general of the Big Death Stars' Sun Army. It leads a force comprised of Beast and Beast Man Digimon that have adapted to and Digivolved through a variety of environments. However, their motives are unknown.
　This Digimon was the result of a bug originating from Apollomon's suppressed inner belligerence and explosive sense of pride, which caused it to split and created this Digimon. The blue flames surrounding it are hotter and more frenzied than those of Apollomon, but its technique also lacks precision.
　Its special move Arrow of Whispered fires off blue flames in the form of arrows from the balls of light in its hands. With Sun of Sorrows, it hurls an enormous black and blue ball of fire at its enemies.

name: KingWhamon
background:
The Mega form of Whamon. It has built a habitable island upon itself for the Digimon who often ride in its mouth while it is traveling through net space. KingWhamon is also able to protect others while at sea by shielding the entire island in a giant bubble. If there is ever a ruckus on the island, KingWhamon is able to forcefully eject those responsible by spouting from its blowhole.
Its special moves are Aqua Jet, in which KingWhamon attacks with a burst of highly-pressurized water, and Cranial Bash, where it bashes its enemies with its rock-hard head.

name: Armamon:Burst Mode
background:
This is the form Armamon takes on when it ascends and transforms into a swordsman, enabling it to draw from its secret collection of powerful swords.
　While its weapon transformation abilities have been lost, the four large arms on its back extend to wield a variety of weapons and utilize different swordfighting techniques according to the situation.
　There is record of one obtaining incredible power after merging with a special system.
　With Sword of Meteor, it unleashes a powerful slicing attack with its six swords, and with Infinity Sword Rain, it brings the swords floating around it crashing down over its opponent's head.

name: Armamon
background:
A Weapon Digimon considered to be one of the Legend-Arms and is capable of transforming its own body into a weapon.
　That power is not limited to its own transformation; this Digimon is also capable of forcibly taking the bodies of Digimon it has sliced through with its enormous sword and transforming them into weapons. Seeing as there are no other beings with such a power, it is thought that this Digimon is tied to the origins of the Legend-Arms.
　Armamon is very harsh in its assessment of whether those who might wield it are worthy or not. It will not entrust its body to anyone with less strength than the Royal Knights.
　It fights by showering its opponents in sharp crystals with Malkut Crystal, or by using Rainbow Hand to render them unconscious and fire a rainbow-colored aura from its right hand. It displays brilliant swordsmanship with its special move, Sword Masquerade.

name: Shoutmon X5S
background:
The Space Fighter form resulting from the DigiFusion of Shoutmon X5 and a weaponized Spadamon. It possesses both powerful skill with the sword and impressive flight capabilities.
　With Space Chakram, it utilizes cosmic energy to fire off a disc of light, while Helioslasher cloaks its Starsword in a blazing, sunlike veil and slices enemies. With its special move Cosmo Victory, it gathers cosmic energy around Spadamon's blade and attacks.

name: Vortexdramon
background:
A Bird Dragon Digimon regarded as one of the heroes of the Vortex Warriors. Its torso is made up of the energy from turbulent winds. It races through the skies like a gale, shedding light far and wide with its gleaming wings. It has been spotted flying across battlefields, carrying the Digimon once known as the Vortex Commander upon its back.
　While outwardly a ferocious dragon, it also possesses the characteristics of a powerful mage, capable of freely combining the four elements of earth, water, fire, and wind to conjure a wide range of wind magic.
　Its special moves include the absolute-zero Frozen Gust—cold enough to freeze the sea—and Blazing Gust: a scorching wind that incinerates both its target foes and the ground beneath them. Additionally, by transforming its arms into a colossal sword, it can unleash Laevateinn: a slashing attack with whirlwind accompaniment.

name: Shoutmon X4S
background:
This Digimon is the Armed Fighter resulting from the DigiFusion of Shoutmon X4 and Spadamon. By equipping the transformed Spadamon, this Digimon is able to use various sword skills.
　It keeps its opponents in check by firing off bullets from its head with Blaze Fire, and slices to attack enemies with the weapons in both hands with Cross Red & Blue. With its special move Victory Brave, it cloaks the weaponized Spadamon in a blade aura and cuts down its opponent.

name: Shoutmon X3SD
background:
This Digimon is the result of performing DigiFuse on Shoutmon X3 with SkullKnightmon and DeadlyAxemon. This is its Demon God form, which imposingly wields the enormous Victory Spear.
　In its left arm, it wields a weapon resembling the head of a red monster, which fires off a strong gust laced with a potent acid for Acid Typhoon. With its special move Billion Stab, it uses its spear to rapidly and repeatedly pierce its opponent.

name: Shoutmon X3GM
background:
The result of performing DigiFuse on Shoutmon X3 with Greymon and MailBirdramon. Upon taking on the Pterosaur form, it gains wings that emits energy so intense that its heat can be felt even at a distance.
　Shoutmon X3GM is in possession of ferocious skills. It can cloak its entire body in fire energy before taking aim at its target and attacking with Wild Burning, or use Brilliance Dagger to slice down its opponent with its wings of light. With Breath of Perun, it fires off a heat ray wrapped in lightning from its mouth.

name: Azhdarmon
background: A Pterosaur Digimon with three giant blades growing from its body. Azhdarmon is a fearsome predator that lives in cold mountainous regions, reigning as the top beast of prey in the area. With excellent eyesight, it can spot prey unnoticed from extremely high altitudes and plummet down to dispatch it skillfully using the blades growing from its body. As a top predator, it is rarely attacked by other Digimon, however it can often get into fatal conflicts with other Azhdarmon in disputes over territory. When hunting, it uses Tomahawk Strike, transferring the energy from a sudden descent into its blades to kill with a single blow. Tri Carve Rain, an attack that uses the three blades on its head and wings to slice opponents to pieces, and Trinity Lightning, a high voltage lightning attack fired from its blades, are rarely used when hunting prey. These attacks are mostly used to fight enemies that enter its territory.

name: Tengumon
background:
A great Tengu Digimon who lives in the dark depths of the mountains at the head of a band of Karatenmon followers. The Karatenmon train daily under its instruction, with their final trial being to defeat their master. Whoever does so succeeds the current master by Digivolving into the next Tengumon. It is said that as part of the new Tengumon's inauguration, they must flourish a war fan imbued with Genriki and cause disaster to strike in order to announce their succession.
　Its Apostate's Stiletto special move sees Tengumon kick out at its opponent while slashing them with the blades attached to the bottom of its getas. By channeling all of its Genriki into its war fan, Tengumon can also conjure a fiery tornado capable of sweeping up even boulders with just a single wave before releasing it at its target. This attack's destructive power is enough to turn those who oppose it and the vicinity into ashes, leading other mountain-dwelling Digimon to fear Tengumon.

name: Amaterasumon
background:
Amaterasumon is the being who presides over the sun in the eastern parts of the Digital World. The ornaments on its body have caused some to speculate that it may be connected to Susanomon. It resides in a place beyond the clouds called the Divine Realm. In the event of disaster or when it becomes upset, it changes the ornaments on its arms and transforms into its Ama no Iwato form, closing itself off from the outside world—an action which causes the sun system to experience difficulties for the duration of its self-imposed seclusion . 
　Its Izu no Mitama special move allows it to manifest a crimson-bladed sword from its arm ornaments to rend its opponent in two. It can also use its All-Encompassing Sunbeams special move to deploy all the parts on its arms and the ring on its back, enabling it to focus the sun's power to fire off powerful rays of light. Just as the sun bathes all in its light, this special move incinerates every last one of Amaterasumon's enemies, wherever they try to hide.

name: Enmamon
background:
An Evil King Digimon who rules over Yomi, the underworld. Digimon in the eastern Digital World are sent to this realm after death. The bluish-white and red familiars floating by Enmamon's side that resemble human souls are respectively called Zenzu and Akuzu.
　The two inscribe DigiCore information onto scrolls for Enmamon to examine. Should a DigiCore be determined to contain a bug, that DigiCore is confined within Yomi, never to be reincarnated.
　Enmamon's word dictates the laws of Yomi, and it rules with an iron fist, punishing those who attempt to escape. Yet, it too is also bound by the same system, unable to ascend to the outside world. It possesses a mirror capable of reflecting all attacks named the Mirror of Retribution, which makes defeating Enmamon in the underworld impossible.
　Using its special move Yama's Descent, Enmamon swings its ritual baton downward to conjure a giant fist of hellfire above its target, which it then brings crashing down on the target's head.

name: SeitenGokuumon
background:
The long, long journey seeking doctrines of the truth has come to an end. What it obtained was power that was the embodiment of unity.
　SeitenGokuumon is a Tathāgata Digimon that ascended to Digivolution through a new power, attained from scriptures revealed by Sanzomon via the bond with its traveling companions Cho-Hakkaimon and Sagomon.
　Though its fundamental good nature has not changed, it has abandoned its reckless ways and has grown to become a warrior that fights on instinct while also displaying composure.
　SeitenGokuumon's spirit was not all that ascended to godhood. The Kintoun, the golden embellishments on its back, can be made to transform into storm clouds. Its trusty Nyoikinkobou has absorbed power from SeitenGokuumon's companions, and combined with Gokuumon's abilities to become the Nyoitenjinbou, capable of freely transforming and harnessing a trio of elements—lightning, fire, and water.
　With its ability to control magic and use martial arts, it is certainly a powerful Digimon fit to be called a mighty warrior.
　SeitenGokuumon is a Digimon of immense strength that rivals the Tentei Hachibushu who serve as guardians of the eastern regions of the Digital World. However, it is a free-spirited creature which will not be bound by the obligations of its destiny, which draws a weary eye from Erlangmon.　
　For its special moves, SeitenGokuumon releases a flame-like flash of light to momentarily increase its strength in its Fiery Eye. For Hakkai Scorcher, it spouts flames from the Nyoitenjinbou. Its staff makes a fierce whirlpool that swallows the enemy in Gojo Water Tomb. Lastly, the Nyoitenjinbou transforms into a bazooka and gathers lightning before firing its Supreme Lightning Cannon.

name: ExMaquinamon
background:
A unique Digimon that simultaneously possess two DigiCores—one beast and one machine—within a single body. Its body is composed of data with differing natures on its left and right sides, separated clean down the middle.
　It wields the power of Escaton, which can forcibly fuse disparate elements into one. This allows for the use of a wide range of techniques, such as impacting data streams from its left and right hands to trigger explosions, or forcibly fusing incompatible data with an enemy to trigger damage as that data is then rejected by the host.
 ExMaquinamon remains an enigma, with additional details yet to be uncovered.

name: Dalphomon
background:
Let those who have fallen from heaven fear the beast. With gold fur covering its giant body and a pair of beautiful wings contrary to it's otherwise horrifying appearance, Dalphomon is a Mega Digimon that behaves like an Angel Digimon despite being a Beast Digimon.
　Spends most of its time within cumulonimbus clouds drifting across the sky, rarely concerning itself with other Digimon.
　It reveres the beautiful physical forms of beasts and believes it can reach a higher state of being by absorbing the uncontaminated data of Angel and Holy Beast Digimon. For this reason, it hates Machine and Cyborg Digimon above all else as they do not hesitate to modify their bodies.
　With its special move, "Prophet of Elijah", it fires destructive rays that tracks its enemy using green gems in its head and forelegs. Using "Jailmaon" allows it to grab Digimon with a claw on the tip of it's tail and seal them eternally inside its body without sending them to the Dark Area. "Brutal Halo" amplifies the extreme heat of its breath attack made through its Bone Halo, searing away all configuration data except for the DigiCore.

name: Shishimamon
background:
A Holy Beast Digimon who can absorb darkness and transform it into light. On occasion, the sheer radiance of this act has lit up the entire Digital World, making night appear as day; this has led to Shishamon becoming known as "That Which Devours Night." Although this ability suggests it is able to Digivolve into a Mega possessing holy powers, it is also feared for its potential to become an evil Digimon as a result of all the darkness it consumes.
　Shishamon performs its Kagura Flash special move by using its shining, light-imbued claws to rip its enemies apart with graceful dancing motions. It can also use its special move Evil-Quelling Effulgence to change the entirety of its absorbed darkness into light and shoot it out as giant bullets, which are said to purge any and all evils that they touch.

name: Darumamon
background:
An unidentified Mutant Digimon that wears a daruma costume. The streamlined shape of its suit deflects all attacks, and is specially designed to prevent the wearer from tumbling over. Gentle by nature, Darumamon is often found sitting in quiet meditation—however, if you make one hostile, it displays an almost-ridiculous level of strength. A small number have been confirmed to exist on an island in the eastern Digital World.
　Darumamon can use its Daruma Fire Charge special move to cover its body in fire before ramming into its enemy. It can also use its Daruma Execution special move to sever the body of any opponent that enters its barrier into many pieces, no matter how tough it might be. This move parts head from torso, making it lethal to any who experience it.

name: Rhamphomon
background: A Pterosaur Digimon with large membrane-like wings. Rhamphomon has excellent flying ability and can even carry Digimon larger than itself with both feet while in the air. Launching itself from the ground straight up into the sky, it glides around on air currents, spending most of the day soaring in the air. When it spots prey during its flight, it tucks its wings beneath itself and dives down at once in a swooping attack. When hunting, Rhamphomon stuns its prey using Crystal Cannon to shoot ultra high-velocity crystal shells from it's mouth or Hammer Strike to bludgeon with the crystal on the tip of its tail. Following this, it seizes its prey with both feet and carries it high into the air before using No Hope Fall as a finisher, dropping it and slamming it into the ground. Some specimens can use an offshoot technique called Crystal Rain in which Crystal Cannon is fired twice in succession, causing the crystal shells to collide in mid-flight and shatter into smaller fragments.

name: Chronomon: Destroy Mode
background:
The form that Chronomon takes after going berserk. A monster of legend that is said to have abandoned its responsibility to maintain the stability of space-time, instead rebelling against the systems that control time in the Digital World.  While Chronomon was attempting to repair space-time, the malice that exists in the Digital World simultaneous flowed into it, corrupting it like a virus and causing it to lose control. Chronomon: Destroy Mode abuses its innate ability to control time, hacking into the memories engraved in the Digital World itself and downloading a diverse array of moves which it uses to cause mayhem.
　The special move Finger Bomb forcibly restores the past data of a foe that Chronomon points at, causing them to explode from the colossal amount of data swelling within their body. Additionally, even in this out-of-control state, Chronomon can still use Holy Flare, which incinerates its enemies.
　Finally, Chrono Devolution converts the chronological data of the Digital World into energy and unleashes it. Those struck by this attack age in the blink of an eye, their data reduced to mere debris.

name: Chronomon: Holy Mode
background:
A Deity Digimon said to be the manifestation of systems that preside over time.  The flow of time within the Digital World is nebulous and has often been disrupted by clashes between powerful Digimon and even natural disasters. Chronomon is tasked with repairing time and space in the case of such events. When mending time-based anomalies, Chronomon also removes the very instance of its manifestation from reality, meaning that none have ever witnessed its form. It is for this reason that the existence of Chronomon is only ever briefly touched on in legends.
　Its special moves are Holy Flare, a sacred flame that incinerates the very existence of those who would maliciously corrupt space-time, and Chronos Crop, an unguardable attack in which Chronomon uses its wings like blades to sever the enemy along with time and space.

name: Butenmon
background:
A Deity Digimon donning crimson armor and wielding a massive sword. Digivolved from a Digimon that has honed its skill with the blade through countless battles. Though still an Ultimate-level Digimon, Butenmon's mastery is already approaching the limits of swordsmanship. It wields its massive sword with both elegant finesse and enough brute strength to shatter mountains. The image of Butenmon rushing into battle, blade in hand, summons to mind the very definition of a mighty warrior.
　The special move, Sunburst Dance, traces a circle with a high-speed swing of its great blade, slicing through foes along its arc. Its other special move, Daybreak Flash, is a powerful strike, charged with enough force to level a mountain. Those who've witnessed Butenmon defeat its enemies with this attack have likened it to a war god shining light upon a dark battlefield.

name: Buraimon
background:
A Bird Man Digimon that Digivolved through diligent swordsmanship training. This training granted it the prowess necessary to wield two blades with great skill. Buraimon utilizes its polished skills as a sword for hire while wandering the Digital World on a journey to grow ever stronger. Rumors that suggest Buraimon will take on any job for a reward, no matter how nefarious, have lead to it frequently being regarded as a ruffian. However, in truth, Buraimon possesses a kind personality and will never turn away those in need of help, outright refusing any job that would require harming the weak.
　Buraimon's special moves include Dual Swallow Reversal, which shreds foes using rapid slices unleashed with both its blades in the shape of a cross, and Secret Sword: Raptor Felling, a spinning counter slash that launches Buraimon up to strike opponents in flight.

name: Hyokomon
background:
A Baby Bird Digimon that Digivolved based on data regarding samurai.  It trains day and night to perfect the way of the sword, developing new secret sword techniques. Though Hyokomon displays confidence in its swordsmanship, in truth, its actual strength yet lags behind. It's said that the moment its rigorous training leads to breaking out of its shell is the very moment that Hyokomon is ready to Digivolve.
　When performing the special move, Downward Cleave, Hyokomon uses its wings and legs to leap high over its opponent, bringing its sword down upon their head. Any left standing are quickly knocked out by Hyokomon's Secret Sword: Chirping Slash, a techinque devised through countless training sessions.

name: Chicchimon
background:
A Baby Bird Digimon whose entire body is covered in fuzzy, yellow down. Because it cannot yet fly, it hops along the ground while vigorously flapping its little wings. Chicchimon's trademark is its long crest, and it is frequently witnessed using its tiny beak to obsessively groom this important feature.
　When Chicchimon senses danger, it will unleash the special move, Sonic Chirp, an ultrasonic cry that can even knock out a Rookie Digimon at point-blank range. Chicchimon will then make a quick escape to safety while its aggressor is stunned.

name: ZombiePlutomon
background:
With the functions of its DigiCore halted, Plutomon—a Digimon that typically enjoys a long life—has become a strange being whose body is simply going through the motions. It no longer possesses any free will, nor does it punish evil, having lost its sense of purpose too. Cursed by its undying DigiCore to wander endlessly, it can no longer contemplate anything but the indiscriminate consumption of others' data. A fearsome predator in a completely different sense when it was alive, ZombiePlutomon's decaying flesh ensures that its hunger will never be sated, no matter how many DigiCores it devours.
In its special move Hazardous Hold, ZombiePlutomon absorbs data from its prey's DigiCore until they decay. With Hell Slicer, it launches sickle-shaped X-particles from its left hand to carve up enemies, and finally, when using Marionette abomination, ZombiePlutomon bites foes and infects them with its own virus, turning them into puppets it can manipulate.

name: Manekimon
background:
A Puppet Digimon that has assimilated data on the maneki-neko. Contrary to its cute appearance, a punch from its oversized right arm can shatter slabs of rock. The device attached to Manekimon's right paw serves to attract things towards it, but instead of bringing good fortune, the beckoning motions are used to lure in and attack victims.
　Manekimon's special moves include Koban Toss, with which it can make koban—oval gold coins—appear from its paws before throwing them as projectiles. Its Welcoming Fist special move couples its right paw device's power of attraction with its raw punching strength, and can easily knock out even mightier Digimon with a clean hit.

name: Hakubamon
background:
A Holy Beast Digimon possessing features from both horses and eastern dragons, including sharp claws, whiskers, and scales. Hakubamon lives alone in mountainous regions, without forming groups. Its platinum fur and draconic scales repel fire, and have helped it in when rescuing In-Training and Rookie Digimon that were late to escape from wildfires. Due to its proud disposition, it seldom lets anyone on its back. However, there have reportedly been sightings of Hakubamon allowing Sanzomon onto its back before heading off to places of its own will.
　Its special moves include Severing Dragon Claw, with which it rends enemies with its sharp, golden claws, and Blazing Beads, with which it launches the beads around its neck at its foe. The beads pursue them for as long as evil intent resides in their heart, beating and burning them to submission.

name: Wankomon
background:
A friendly, hardworking Beast Digimon who displays fierce loyalty to the Digimon or Tamer it sees as its master. Although often seen sunbathing, Wankomon remains ever-alert, gleaning information about its surroundings with its heightened sense of smell. It is always playing with the ball it carries around, and is delighted whenever it finds someone willing to play with it.
　Wankomon's special moves include Crushing Ball, in which it charges its ball with mysterious powers and uses its tail to throw it at the enemy, before causing it to greatly expand above their head and crush them. When it senses danger, it can use Stone Body to harden its body, defending itself against even stronger Digimon.

name: Gasamon
background:
A mysterious Mollusk Digimon with distinctive tentacles who wears a wickerwork hat much like a shell, within which it often conceals its weapon. Although it normally disguises itself as a wickerwork hat, when an unsuspecting Digimon approaches, it launches a surprise attack in an attempt to prey on the victim. For this reason, it frequently clashes with BushiAgumon, a Digimon that gets lured in by its desire to wear the hat. However, Gasamon will lose in a direct sword fight, so it quickly runs away if its surprise attack fails.
　Gasamon's special move Wickerwork Whirlwind sees it perform wild slices as it spins its entire body—hat included—round and round. Its Acidic Mucus special move, in which it spits out a dissolving liquid from its normally-hidden mouth, is thought to be used to melt down victims it has captured for easier consumption.

name: Takinmon
background:
A Beast Digimon with distinctive golden fur and bull-like features that lives in mountainous areas. Traces of psychokinetic energy course through its golden fur, protecting it against both stormy weather and enemy attacks. Takinmon's horns can generate psychokinetic energy to levitate boulders or other objects, glowing gold when they do so. Aside from offensive purposes, it also uses this energy to forge paths through the mountains. It is said it sometimes accidentally makes Gotsumon float while doing so, leading to fights.
　In its Horn Rush special move, Takinmon charges at its opponent to gore them with its horns. It can also use Rock Missile to fire off boulders with psychokinetic energy; the boulders continue to home in on the target even if they manage to dodge, making this attack extremely difficult to defend against.

name: Fujamon
background:
A voracious Aquatic Digimon that lives in the various rivers of the eastern Digital World. Aggressive by nature, when another Digimon trespasses into its territory, Fujamon will use its venomous fangs to try and drive away the intruder, no matter how strong it might be. It is able to dexterously use its large fins to grab onto objects or crawl on land, albeit only for a short period of time. It rotates its long, snake-like tail like a propeller when swimming, allowing it to venture upstream. This trait is proposed as one reason for its wide range of habitats.
　Fujamon's Hydro Blast special move sees it violently shake its tail fin, shooting off beads of water like a shotgun. It can also use Serpentine Fang to deliver bites with its venomous fangs that can even paralyze Champion and Ultimate Digimon.

name: Kakamon
background:
A Beast Digimon clad in beautiful red and gold fur. Kakamon takes special care of its trademark, mohawk-like hair atop its head. It can been found in various habitats throughout vast regions of the eastern Digital World. Kakamon has an upbeat nature, enjoying singing and music, and will proudly make other Digimon it encounters listen to its songs.
A certain other Digimon, Pandamon, is popular in the eastern parts of the Digital World, and there are many Kakamon that yearn for the lifestyle of such a lone wolf, as shown by the Pandamon sticker on their headphones.
　For its special moves, Kakamon curls its body up like a ball and smashes into its opponent for Monkey Ball. In its Golden Howl, it makes use of its well-trained voice, shouting and releasing sound waves that pummel its foe.

name: Armalizamon
background: A Reptile Digimon covered head to toe in tough scales. To maintain the hardness of its scales, this Digimon eats high-density minerals, and thus, often resides around mineral deposits. Once it has extracted all the ore from one location, it roams around in search of a new mineral vein. The ore consumed by Armalizamon is broken down into particles within an internal disintegration furnace before being converted into energy via the vein-like particle accelerator that runs throughout its entire body. This energy is consumed in maintaining the hardness of its scales and to power its attacks. The charged neutrons generated by its internal particle accelerator are fired from its mouth with Neutron Laser, an attack that can pierce through virtually anything. At full output, this laser is strong enough to even cut through Ultimate-level Digimon. Additionally, its Neutron Diffuser ability fires scattered beams that can target a wide area, albeit with decreased power. In close combat, Armalizamon burns through enemies with its Neutron Blade attack: blade-shaped beams of charged neutrons emitted by extending the scales on its arms. Due to its diet of high-density ore, a full stomach makes Armalizamon's body become extremely heavy. In this state, it can use the Death Roll Press ability to curl up and ram into enemies, unleashing its maximum destructive potential. Armalizamon must consume sufficient amounts of ore to use all of its attacks to their full effect. As a result, when at their mineral deposit feeding grounds, these Digimon are aggressive, whereas those wandering in search of mineral veins prefer to avoid unnecessary fights due to a lack of energy.

name: Onibimon
background:
A Ghost Digimon that assumes the form of a small spirit. As its appearance suggests, it is able to slip through tangible surfaces. In eastern parts of the Digital World, Onibimon are believed to be Digimon that lived to the end of their natural lives, reincarnated by passing through the world of Yomi.
　Its special move, Ghastly Flames, sees Onibimon blow bluish-white sparks at its opponent.

name: Tomorimon
background:
A Ghost Digimon that floats through the air covered in a gaseous veil, constantly emanating a faint glow. Known for appearing at night, it has been sighted in areas throughout the eastern parts of the Digital World, glimmering as it wanders the dark.
　When it senses danger, it uses Will-o'-the-Wisp to illuminate the gases enshrouding its body, blinding enemies.

name: Mococomon
background:
A Smoke Digimon that Digivolved from Fukamon, losing its tendency to drift with the wind while growing fangs and becoming more active. In the eastern Digital World, Mococomon is revered as the origin of all Digivolution, and even aggressive Digimon rarely attempt to do it harm.
　Despite this, it still finds itself in danger from time to time. When threatened, it uses Viscous Clouds to breathe out sticky cumulus clouds, encumbering its aggressor's movements before escaping.

name: Fukamon
background:
A Smoke Digimon who, like a cloud, doesn't contain much matter and floats through the air. Fukamon rarely moves on its own accord and instead lets the wind carry it whichever way it blows. As such, individuals can be found all over the continents in the eastern Digital World. During stormy weather, it is said to take shelter under objects or larger Digimon so that it doesn't get blown away.
　When it feels threatened, it uses Cloudcall to breathe out clouds, then flees.

name: Parallelmon
background: A Mutant Digimon capable of freely traveling to and from Digital Worlds of other dimensions. Its objective shrouded in mystery, this fiendish existence devours the data of Digimon that inhabit the worlds it travels to. Parallelmon emerges out of nowhere like a phantom, and the great chaos and confusion it wreaks has led research teams to classify its appearance as a natural disaster. When using the special move Absorbent Bang, Parallelmon lets forth a beam of light from its eye that absorbs any enemy it strikes. Anything that hits the beam other than the target is sent to another dimension. Furthermore, Parallelmon can use Endless Trance to open countless portals around its target and launch unilateral attacks from all directions.

name: NightChiropmon
background: A Beast Man Digimon that wears a full-body stealth suit. While this Digimon specializes in nighttime reconnaissance and information-gathering, it also excels at close-quarters combat using agile martial arts. The suit covering its entire body is anti-reflective, muffles sound, and suppresses body heat emissions. This makes NightChiropmon nearly impossible to detect when operating at night. Its helmet is equipped with ultrasonic echolocation capabilities and a sensor that can detect the full electromagnetic spectrum, so nothing within its surroundings goes unobserved. NightChiropmon's abilities seem geared toward military applications, suggesting it may have imported leaked developmental data from a certain mechanized brigade of the Digital World when Digivolving. In combat, NightChiropmon will first use its helmet's full capabilities when performing Providence Scan to analyze its target down to their internal data. Once the analysis is complete, it uses Giant Rang to unleash a ranged slashing attack with the massive boomerang on its back, or moves in close and pinpoints its target's weak points with the ultra quick strikes and kicks of its Shadow Arts ability. Additionally, NightChiropmon uses Mesmer Burst to fire ultrasonic waves from its fingertips, which cause temporary paralysis. It can send out these waves from all fingers of both hands.

name: Styracomon
background:
A powerful, heavyweight fighter whose bulk rivals that of UltimateBrachiomon. Styracomon boasts sturdy armor and muscles, as well as the ability to generate an energy field using the horns over its body— an impressive array of defenses that render physical attacks futile.
 Styracomon prides itself on its ability to withstand incoming attacks thanks to its impenetrable defense. When it goes on the offensive, the special moves with which it retaliates are deadly. With Crimson Crest, it concentrates energy into the many horns on its head armor before letting loose a destructive beam. With Dreadnought Stampede, it launches missiles from all over its body while charging at its foes, annihilating all in its wake.
 The valiant way Styracomon shrugs off attacks only to counter with greater force has earned the admiration of many Digimon, and has even attracted a slew of Digimon looking for a challenge with which to test the strength they take pride in.

name: Taotiemon
background:
A Demon Digimon covered in sheep-like fur that dons a bronze decoration like a helmet . Don't be fooled by its young appearance—its innocent face belies its true nature as a mischievous child who loves playing immature and often cruel pranks.
　Taotiemon can often be seen drifting, cloudlike, through the skies of Jakeizan. With a possible bug in its data causing it to be constantly starving, it attempts to feast as soon as it happens upon prey. When it feeds, the decoration on its head opens up to reveal a horrifying maw.
　The seals affixed to Taotiemon's head sentence it to an inescapable confinement within the bounds of Jakeizan. Because of this, much like Dijangmon, it is thought to be one of the Shikyoju—four inauspicious beasts that have existed in the eastern parts of the Digital World since ancient times—and is presumed to have been sealed within the bounds of the mountain by an immensely powerful being.
　Taotiemon's special moves include Gorge Horns, in which the giant maw on its head sucks in and swallows prey whole, and it can use  Vile Vomit to spit out leftovers from the vast amounts of data it has devoured, showering enemies with acid. Lastly, its Voracious Blast allows it to expel the energy amassed in its body from its excessive eating to trigger an extremely powerful explosion, although this comes with the drawback of stimulating its hunger even more acutely than usual.

name: Craniamon + Enbarrmon
background: A small number of Royal Knights may sometimes conscript the Digimon in their environs to help accomplish their mission. Their methods in recruiting allies depends upon the mission at hand, but Craniamon will obtain allies by challenging a Digimon it recognizes as strong to a duel and forcing it to submit. This mounted form is adopted by Craniamon during a mission after defeating Enbarrmon and commandeering it to use as their horse. Always a perfectionist, it is believed that Craniamon set its eyes on Enbarrmon's high mobility in order to more efficiently accomplish its tasks. While they have fought against each other in many a fierce battle, recognizing their respective strengths and forging a strong bond of trust with one another has enabled them to develop astonishing levels of mobility and offensive prowess. With their Ruthless Charge special move, Enbarrmon dashes at full speed for a piercing attack with the Claíomh Solais lance. Enbarrmon's lightning-fast thrust uses the masses of both Digimon, and with Craniamon's brute strength behind it, most other Digimon hit by the attack will be obliterated into nothingness. In addition, the "never die" protection that Enbarrmon grants to those who ride it affects Craniamon's magical shield Avalon. While mounted, the three-second time limit of the omni-directional Breath of the Gods defensive maneuver is removed, and all attacks are permanently nullified.

name: Enbarrmon
background:
An Undead Digimon that races across the seas and through the skies as easily as it gallops on land. Enbarrmon is wild and extremely aggressive in nature. Since it does not move in a herd, it is also a rare sight. In fitting with its Undead nature, the supreme protection Enbarrmon offers its rider ensures that they will "never die," but it is said that only those Enbarrmon deems worthy may mount it. Additionally, the shock wave generated when Enbarrmon reaches top speed deals catastrophic damage to its surroundings.
In its special move, Dead Angle Horn, it charges out of the sky as if appearing from its foe's blind spot, while for Vain Answer, it counters its adversary's attacks with mighty blows from its hind legs.

name: Metatromon
background:
This iron giant assumes the airs of a vengeful angel. While its imposing stature and angelic form make it easy to mistake for an Angel Digimon, it has no holy power whatsoever, and is in truth a Machine Digimon to its core.
Its appearance also belies its arrogance: it looks down on others, believing itself the most refined of all Machine Digimon and deserving of standing above the rest. It has the strength to back up this belief, which only adds to its unpleasant nature.
　The wing-like parts surrounding it are highly versatile autonomous weapons, with functions ranging from tracking enemies when firing lasers to creating a protective energy field. Moreover, its gleaming armor is extremely durable, and the eyes covering its body give it a 360-degree field of view.
　Although its own movements are sluggish, it has a flexible fighting style thanks to its autonomous weapons capable of overwhelming opponents.
　Metatromon's special move Iron Halo allows it to freeze its opponent's movements using magnetic waves emitted from the iron ring floating above its head. It can also use Eyes of Enoch to fire lasers from the many eyes covering its body, and Metatronios to obliterate enemies by surrounding them with its wing-like autonomous weapons and energy fields.

name: GreatGrizzlymon
background:
The self-proclaimed greatest martial artist in the Digital World, this Digimon dedicates itself to entertaining spectators of its battles. Its performance in battle captivates the Digimon watching and enlivens the battlefield. GreatGrizzlymon works hard and never neglects its training, all so that it can meet the audience's expectations. Matches against its arch-rival, PileVolcanomon, have become major crowd-drawing events.
Its special moves include Spirited Claws, an attack that tears through foes with claws engulfed in flames, and Incendiary Mist, an explosion caused by igniting the poison mist GreatGrizzlymon spews from its mouth. However, the crowd-favorite move is GGS (Grizzly Gigantic Strike), where GreatGrizzlymon summons a wrestling ring from the ground before delivering a powerful lariat at the speed of sound.

name: Titamon+SkullBaluchimon
background:
SkullBaluchimon, believed to possess no emotions, has been tamed and restrained by Titamon. Having addressed its sole weakness—a lack of mobility—Titamon can now roam battlefields freely, in a manner befitting its title of demon god. Typically, anything that comes into contact with the cold, dark energy flowing from SkullBaluchimon's DigiCore will be rendered powerless by an overwhelming fear of death. Titamon, however, has absorbed SkullBaluchimon's power into its own through sheer force of will. The Zanjinto also combines with SkullBaluchimon's data to transform from a large blade into a giant double-edged naginata. The grudge-laden data billowing forth from the Zanjinto is strengthened further by SkullBaluchimon's cold, dark energy, ensuring that the wounds it deals leave marks that never heal on victims' bodies and souls alike.
For Titamon's special move, Senjin Kishinzan, it spins the giant Zanjinto-naginata, slicing at the enemy's DigiCore and grinding it into dust.

name: Cougarmon
background: A Mammal Digimon whose stature resembles that of a wildcat. Though incredibly rare, Cougarmon has been confirmed to live in the mountainous regions of the eastern Digital World. Covered in a dense fur that allows it to endure the frigid mountain peaks, it is also highly resistant to attacks from external threats. Many of these Digimon appear to have gentle personalities, but they will also aggressively eliminate any threats that trespass into their territory. After inflicting damage in a single ferocious swipe with Ruthless Claw, Cougarmon will use Ruthless Fang to bite onto its foe's neck with its sharp teeth and deliver the finishing blow. At times, it will also unleash a feral howl with Wrath Roar as a warning to those that enter its territory.

name: MasterBlimpmon
background:
An Airship Digimon that soars through the heavens. The previously incomplete Blimpmon Digivolved into MasterBlimpmon via improvements and remodeling. Now complete, its formerly exposed balloon has been equipped with armor. Its finalized armor has reduced air resistance, increasing MasterBlimpmon's cruising speed. This allows it to deliver its passenger Digimon swiftly to the battlefield.
For its Zeppelin Circus move, MasterBlimpmon shoots a bombardment of guided missiles from its many gunports. During Daredevil Break, it crashes at breakneck speed into enemy encampments, ramming them with the drill on its bow.

name: Hyemon
background: A Beast Digimon with sharp fangs and claws. It boasts a large, powerful physique, unusual for most Rookie Digimon. Hyemon tend to live in groups, and many hunt in packs to secure food efficiently. They relentlessly pursue their prey using their cunning and high stamina, making packs of Hyemon highly effective hunters. A Hyemon's jaw is so powerful it is able to chew and consume things even as hard as metal. One of its special moves is Curse Bite, a gnawing attack that makes use of its powerful jaws and sharp fangs. And when using Release Howling, a Hyemon lets out a ferocious roar that temporarily heightens the physical abilities of nearby Hyemon. This means that a pack howling in unison will amplify the effect and grant them enormous power.

name: Murasamemon
background: There is a style of swordsmanship that has been passed down since ancient times in the eastern Digital World. Established in perilous mountain ranges, this style is known as "Meido-Uryu" and is distinctive for its sword techniques with names containing the meaning of rain. It is said that Digimon who complete the grueling training of this style will Digivolve into Murasamemon as proof of their mastery of Meido-Uryu. There are likely very few Digimon who can withstand the ferocious techniques Murasamemon unleashes with its enormous sword, which it wields with effortless strength and skill. Murasamemon uses three special sword arts depending on the situation in battle. Known as the "forceful" blade that cuts through even the sturdiest defenses, Gonozan: Murashigure is a single, cleaving strike from a high stance. Next is Jyunozan: Tsukishigure, the "flexible" blade that can deflect any attack from a low stance. The third move is Tsuinozan: Koku, the "final" blade. Resting the sword on its right shoulder, Murasamemon leaps toward the enemy, spinning vertically in the air to increase its power before delivering a deadly strike. Legend says that those defeated by a Meido-Uryu sword technique experience visions of thunderous rain in their final moments.

name: Wolvermon
background: An Android Digimon that harbors a variety of weapons in both of its arms. These weapons are made of various metals, and the moving parts are said to use Chrome Digizoid. While Wolvermon normally has a calm and quiet personality, heightened focus during battle causes its spirits to surge, leading it to fall into a bloodthirsty frenzy. Wolvermon has a well-rounded arsenal of both long- and close-range attacks. It can engage in battle from a distance by using Rapid Burst to quickly fire energy shots from the muzzles in its palms, or by using Scatter Rocket to destroy its foes with a barrage of mini rockets housed in both arms. When things look bad in a long-range fight, Wolvermon can use its graceful combat skills to swiftly close the distance. Once in range, it can use Tomboy Blaze to unleash flames from its palms and restrain its target before using Badger Slash to deliver the finishing blow with its sharp claws.

name: Chiropmon
background: A Mammal Digimon that resembles a bat wearing a hood. Highly skilled at information gathering and analysis, it is rumored to have been born from software used by an intelligence agency. Able to flap its wings almost soundlessly, Chiropmon can gather information and analyze its target without being noticed. However, since it only gathers information about things it has an interest in, most of what it collects is no more valuable than general knowledge and trivia. Using the ultrasonic waves emitted from its mouth, Chiropmon can unleash Paralyze Echo to paralyze foes, or survey its surroundings via echolocation with Analyze Echo. Curious Eyes allows Chiropmon to "see" beyond the visible spectrum of light. With the ability to view things in ultraviolet and infrared, it can perceive information that is normally invisible. Chiropmon harbors little to no desire to fight and will not start a confrontation. However, when a battle is unavoidable, it can use Condensed Echo to compress ultrasonic waves to their limit before releasing them in an offensive attack.

name: Pristimon
background: A Puppet Digimon with a body made from soft fabric. The pleasant texture of its body and cute appearance may leave you with the urge to give it a cuddle, but carelessly touching this Digimon is dangerous due to its violent nature. Its distinctive tail houses an energy generator, which creates the power it needs to operate by spinning four-stage generator coils. One of its special moves is Pri Shot, which fully unleashes the energy from its generator and fires it out of the muzzles in its palms. With Stab Claw, Pristimon uses the sharp claws on its metal gauntlets to pierce foes while consuming very little energy. Finally, Tail Slap is a strike delivered with Pristimon's tail while the generator coils spin at full power. This move is destructive enough to blow right through a target.

name: Dullahamon
background:
A Ghost Digimon that rides Cóiste Bodhar, its ethereal, trusty horse, in search of Digimon close to death to end their lives.
　Its head and body are linked by chains, and display fearsome coordination in combat despite each possessing minds of their own. However, there have also been sightings of its rampaging head seemingly leading the body as it gallops through the night.
　Its special moves are Bercilak Edge, in which it swings its head like a flail to deliver a barrage of strikes, and Sleepy Hollow, in which it drags enemies into a floating coffin using a spectral hand. A grim fate awaits those who become trapped by the coffin—their physical data is deconstructed, and they become assimilated into the mass of spirits that enshrouds Dullahamon.

name: GEKKOMON
background: A vibrantly-colored Reptile Digimon that inhabits tropical rainforests throughout the Digital World. These bright colors act as camouflage allowing Gekkomon to blend in with its habitat, making spotting it difficult. The pads on its fingers and toes are capable of clinging to all kinds of surfaces, such as the trees or boulders on which it typically resides. Although personality varies from individual to individual, most are of an inquisitive and cheerful nature. Gekkomon's primary special move is Smash Beat, where it strikes foes with its freely-extendable tongue with a force that can even knock out Digimon stronger than itself if it lands a clean hit. It can also utilize its tongue to execute other creative attacks such as Break Throw, in which it grabs nearby objects with its tongue and hurls them at its opponent, or Swing Tackle, in which it uses its tongue to latch onto a nearby object before spinning itself around using centrifugal force to launch a body slam. When backed into a corner, Gekkomon has a tendency to cut off its own tail, which distracts foes while it escapes. Among members of the species, a shortened tail is regarded as a mark of cowardice, whereas a longer and unblemished tail is a sign of the owner's strength.

name: Kekkomon
background: A Lesser Digimon with a multicolored body. Kekkomon's tongue, a highly-evolved sensory organ, is constantly protruding from its mouth. Whenever an object catches its attention, a single lick  allows it to determine detailed information such as its shape, texture, taste, and even its compositional matter. However, possibly due to its developed tongue, its eyesight pales in comparison. Its limbs, although short, allow for nimble movement, making capturing it no easy feat. When attacked by other Digimon, it uses Beat Lick to strike them with its tongue, then uses the time they're recoiling to scurry away in the blink of an eye.

name: Kekomon
background: A Slime Digimon with a vibrantly-colored body. Although its body may be squishy and flexible, its three horns are especially tough for a Slime Digimon and serve as an effective defense against external threats. When it senses danger, it spits out Adhesive Bubble Blow, then escapes while enemies are distracted.

name: Magneticdramon
background:
A Digimon whose body is clad in an armor-like layer of hard rock. Although classified as a Rock Dragon, Magneticdramon is actually a unique Digimon whose structural data does not comprise any matter. Its body, which looks as if it were pure electricity, holds substantial energy reserves and produces a powerful magnetic field. It uses the magnetic pull to attract the rocks forming its body.
The minerals Magneticdramon covers itself with function both as a tough layer of armor and as a weapon. Contrary to its appearance, it is a skillful fighter capable of compensating for its sluggish movements—such as by applying magnetic force onto enemies to pull them in.
　As it is essentially a walking magnetic field, researchers have pointed out that it has a tendency to influence environments of the Digital World. There are concerns that it could have negative effects—such as trigger volcanic activity and tectonic movement—and also impact Digimon ecology, perhaps even causing abnormal Digivolutionary processes.
　Its special moves include Stone Rail Gun, in which it shoots out a high-velocity projectile made from charging the magnets on its body; Electric Compression, which it can use to trap enemies in a powerful magnetic field, crushing them with rocks; and Magnetic Tempest, which delivers a wide-range electromagnetic pulse that temporarily disables its foes' DigiCores and can potentially stun them.

name: Maneuvermon
background:
A Beast Digimon with a unique shape, reminiscent of a fighter jet. Its appearance brings to mind Bird and Bird Dragon Digimon, but unlike most Bird Digimon, it is notable for its bone-hard wings that slice through the air and the jet engine-like organs which enable it to fly. It enjoys giving other Digimon rides in the cockpit-like organ on its neck, but as this requires getting in through its mouth, it often finds that they are not open to the idea.
　As it enjoys flying, it rarely descends from the sky, but on the rare occasions it lands to rest, it exposes the landing gear-like arms hidden within its body before using them to touch down.
　As might be expected from its mastery of flight, it specializes in aerial combat. It also has a wide range of offensive capabilities, such as the ability to discharge electricity from its claws and bionic missiles stowed in its chest.
　Its special move Brutal Aerobatics sees it confuse enemies with acrobatic maneuvers before hitting them with its bionic missiles, and it can use Folgore to fire a barrage of electricity from its claws to scorch its enemies.

name: Turbomon
background:
A quadrupedal Beast Digimon with wheel-like carapaces covering its legs. By rapidly spinning these carapaces while lying prone, it is able to travel in the manner of a motor vehicle, an odd sight sure to stick with anyone who sees it. However, this peculiar ability also grants it a level of mobility impossible for ordinary creatures, such as rapidly moving backwards or making jumps and steep turns.
　It puts this mobility to good use in battle, with powerful accelerated tackles, but is also capable of a more tactical fighting style, by kicking up dust clouds to blind enemies.
　It can rear up on two legs and walk, if only for a short time. However, it rarely does so even outside of battle, as its complete set of shells seems more convenient for movement.
　Its special move Brutal Wheel sees it spin the shells on its front legs at high speed to grind enemies down. It can also spin its entire body at great velocity with Crush Gear Spin before crashing into the enemy, sending it flying.

name: Nyabootmon
background:
A Puppet Digimon whose cape flutters in the wind as it wanders gallantly throughout the Digital World. While it dons the attire of a medieval knight, it is just a cat walking on two legs. Although Nyabootmon might not seem very dignified due to its pompous speech and flamboyant gestures, it actually possesses a strong sense of justice and never condones any acts of evil.
　It practically zips through the air with its tall, gravity-manipulating boots as it journeys across the Digital World, and it relies on its trusty sword, the Catnip Rapier, to deal with any troubles it may encounter during its adventures.
　Despite being of small stature, Nyabootmon is highly proficient at combat. It toys with its foes through its nimble movements and the mesmerizing flourishes of its rapier. The eye-catching way it defeats enemies is nothing short of heroic, and has earned the admiration of many Digimon.
　Its special moves include Gravity Paw, in which it manipulates gravity to jump up high, then rapidly descends to deliver a powerful kick. It can use Carabas Special to quickly close the gap and unleash swift rapier strikes in one fell swoop. In a display of fluid sword strokes and acrobatics, Secret Sword: Nine Lives allows it to disorient its foes and deliver nine slashes in the blink of an eye.

name: Yihumon
background:
A Beast Man Digimon that has the body of a tiger and eagle-like wings sprouting from its arms. It has the ability to control the wind. Yihumon is extremely brutal and cunning. It is brimming with malice by nature.
　Yihumon resides in Jakeizan, a vast and mountainous area of the continent in the eastern regions of the Digital World. It seldom emerges from those darkest peaks, home to many evil spirits. That is why it took a long time to discover. According to observation data, there is a high chance that this Digimon has existed since the earliest times, when the eastern regions of the Digital World emerged.
　The yellow charms on its body are similar to Dijangmon's, and are inscribed with the word "Shikyoju," the four inauspicious beasts, along with data related to ancient seals and wards.
　Yihumon specializes in punches and kicks with kung fu-style movements. Every single one of its attacks is infused with the power of the wind, and every strike is swift and forceful to inflict pain on its opponents.
　For Yihumon's special moves, it delivers a roundhouse kick while firing the razor-sharp Sky Rend. It flaps its wings to fire off countless feathers for Featherblade Breeze. The feathers have been prepared with a poison that dulls the movements of the parts of the body that they hit. Then, the moment the opponent leaves an opening, Yihumon catches them in a twister, and rides the winds while delivering attacks from all directions for Ravaging Storm.

name: Ariemon
background:
An Aquatic Digimon said to be able to cure all ailments and wounds. It has the ability to prophesize calamities or plagues that are to strike the Digital World, and has been sighted delivering warnings throughout various areas. Its body resembles that of a mermaid, but it can float and move on land as well. However, it gradually dries up if it stays away from a body of water.
　Ariemon's hair-like body part is composed of seawater data which can be freely transformed for offensive or defensive purposes, as well as serve as a bed for young Digimon.
　A benevolent soul, it shies away from causing unnecessary harm to its enemies, often content to capture and immobilize them. It also looks after young Aquatic Digimon—such as by conjuring large shells for them to take refuge in—and works to protect the ecosystem.
　Ariemon's special moves are Entangling Algae, in which it extends the ribbon wrapped around its shell staff to immobilize enemies; Tidal Spear, which imbues its staff with seawater and transforms it into a spear to impale its foes; and Cleansing Waters, which summons invigorating seawater to cleanse any wounds or illnesses.

name: SkullSeadramon
background:
The Undead form of Seadramon. Even without any body tissue, it swims on through the Net Ocean, acting on instinct alone as it searches for Digimon to feed on.　Now reduced to its bones, SkullSeadramon has reduced water resistance, enabling it to move at high speeds as it pursues its prey. Upon catching an enemy, SkullSeadramon coils its long body around them, slowly choking their life away with its Death Wrap technique.
For its Lightning Javelin move, it shoots a stinging electric shock from the blade atop its head. The more SkullSeadramon charges this attack, the higher its power becomes.

name: Sharkmon
background:
An enormous shark Digimon that Digivolved as a result of an Aquatic Digimon's voracious appetite for consuming prey. Having absorbed battleship data, more than half of its body is mechanized. The fiendish Sharkmon first uses its anchor to catch Digimon that live in the Net Ocean or pass close by, then consumes them.
For All-Out Bombardment, Sharkmon deploys and fires cannons. It also launches Plutodumon, torpedoes that bite onto their target before self-destructing. Lastly, during Fang Charge, it hooks an enemy on its anchor, then retracts the anchor while rushing forward to attack.

name: Kaguyamon
background:
When looking up at the moonlit night, one might occasionally witness the opening of a gate leading to another world. It is said in the Digital World that when this happens, it is because a Kaguyamon is crying somewhere. Found in beautiful bamboo groves of the eastern Digital World, Kaguyamon has been influenced by the data of a certain princess described in Japanese legend.
　It has a quiet and peaceful nature, acting as guardian of the many mountains that cover the eastern Digital World. It also has the power to open portals to faraway places, even Digital Worlds of other dimensions, enveloping the transported individuals and objects in light to have them appear from a stalk of bamboo at their destination. As it often sends multiple Digimon under its command to areas where anomalous activity has occurred, bamboo groves are said to have formed at such sites.
　Along with the waves of psychic energy emitted from the bamboo-like device on its back, it can manipulate psychokinetic energy, granting it high mobility and the ability to fly. It sometimes uses these powers to handle problems that its subordinates were unable to resolve.
　With its special move Bamboo Reaper, Kaguyamon uses psychokinesis to disarm enemies before raining destruction on the surrounding area in a hail of psychic bullets, and it can use Heavenly Outpour to bombard enemies with extradimensional meteors.

name: Karakurumon
background:
A Puppet Digimon that incorporates data from traditional Japanese mechanical dolls. It wears a large sake cup on its head and resembles a beautiful woman at first glance with its long black hair, but the body underneath the kimono is entirely mechanical. It has been noted to reside together with Kokeshimon in old castles and mansions of the eastern Digital World, with their relationship appearing to be sisterly.
　Hidden beneath its elegant kimono is a multitude of mechanical arms and weapons, which it uses to hunt its prey with the tenacity of an assassin, contrary to its usual serene demeanor. Once it has settled on a target, it deploys its mechanical arms and the wheels on its legs to chase down the enemy, with its mouth agape and fangs bared in a horrifying grin.
　Its special move Hachikazuki Dance sees it skillfully manipulate the weapons beneath its kimono to slash through its enemies, and it can use Acid Deluge to douse enemies with the devastating acid from its bowl, causing them to melt away.

name: Kokeshimon
background:
A Puppet Digimon that Digivolved by incorporating data from a kokeshi, a type of traditional Japanese doll, Kokeshimon often makes its home in the various abandoned castles and mansions scattered across the eastern Digital World.
　Although normally found sitting quietly in one place, to the extent that it can be mistaken for an ornament, it has a mischievous side and enjoys startling those who unwittingly draw near. It also has a caring side, showing a strong drive to protect In-Training and Rookie Digimon, and proves to be a stalwart companion once a bond of trust is established.
　It possesses psychokinetic abilities, and moves by emitting waves of psychic energy from the base of its body, floating from place to place in the manner of a ghost. The soundless nature of its movements makes its approach difficult to notice.
　Kokeshimon can use its special move Sophora Storm to fire a volley of psychokinetic bullets from the base of the kokeshi that make up its arms, while Kideko Strike sees it crash into the ground from on high, unleashing a shockwave of psychic energy that temporarily stuns anything it hits.

name: Hanimon
background:
A Puppet Digimon found mainly in ruin-rich regions of the eastern Digital World. It strongly resembles a haniwa, and much like its namesake, its entire body is made of clay. Although it looks quite hardy as a result, it is more brittle than its appearance would suggest due to being composed of clay data instead of clay itself, and its body is quick to crack and chip. It can be seen steadily working to repair itself whenever this happens.
　While its blank expression can be initially off-putting, it has a mild-mannered nature, and is friendly towards other species. However, it has a strong aversion to the hair on its head being touched, to the point of flying into a rage when this occurs.
　Although weak, it possesses a holy power, and multiple Hanimon gathering to dance can produce a barrier capable of warding off Dark Digimon.
　Its special move, Haniwa Flash, allows it to emit a mysterious light beams from its eyes to both attack and blind enemies, and it can use Tumulus Tackle to collide into its opponent with full force, albeit at the risk of damaging itself.

name: MockingBirdmon
background:
A Machine Digimon modeled after a bird. Its large wings and graceful appearance when flying make it resemble a Bird Digimon, but its head and legs are beak-like robotic arms.　 It can move these arms freely, using them to fire lasers or grab hold of its enemy. The two eye-like devices at the base of its wings also grant it excellent detection capabilities, allowing it to find Digimon hidden in the shadows or underground.
　Along with its physical prowess and strength in battle, it has the ability to record the sounds made by any Digimon it comes across. It uses this aptitude for mimicry when hunting to catch its opponent off guard, displaying a cunning side.
　Once it has spotted a prey, it expertly manipulates its robotic arms to hunt it down with strange movements far removed from its bird-like appearance; it is this command of all the tools at its disposal that make it a true hunter of the sky.
　Its special move Iron Siphon consists in draining the enemy's life force after a puncturing beak attack, while Tri-Airbots sees it spin rapidly, aiming to capture the enemy in its three arms while shooting lasers.

name: Mulemon
background:
A Machine Digimon modeled after a quadrupedal beast. Although its body looks frail, it is actually quite durable, possessing the balance and mobility to handle rough terrain.　 Aside from its four legs, it has a pair of extendable robotic arms folded within its body; these are strong enough to grab and lift things onto its back, or even to send small Digimon flying.
　These characteristics make it particularly suited to carrying things, to the extent that it acts as the courier of the Digital World, ferrying luggage and smaller Digimon to their destinations.
　Owing to its diligent nature, it shows a strong sense of duty in the task of transporting material; however, it has the bad habit of fixating on a single route, and will try to eliminate anything obstructing its path.
　Its special move Iron Gallop makes use of its powerful back legs to deliver a shattering kick, and it can use Missile Rodeo to draw out the missiles stowed on its back and launch them towards the enemy.

name: Maquinamon
background:
A Composite Digimon with both Beast and Machine traits. Although Digimon with mechanical parts are not uncommon, Maquinamon's appearance—perfectly divided between Machine and Beast—is a rarity.
　The balance between these two traits being unstable, it does not often show emotion nor hostility towards opposing Digimon. Its strength in battle is also lacking, making it vulnerable in the cutthroat environment of the Digital World.
　Although lacking in emotion, it is faithful to its instincts and has a tremendous appetite; it is thought that this is due to its instability making its metabolism extremely inefficient.　 However, its animal sense of smell and robotic eyesight make it skilled at finding food, and it is always on the move in search of its next meal. Notably, it is able to change its physiology by gathering data from its surroundings, making it omnivorous and fit for any environment.
　Its special move Twin Bubbles allows it to spit bubbles of fire and electric energy in two rapid bursts. However, these bubbles are barely more powerful than those of In-Training Digimon, making them only useful as a distraction.

name: Nezhamon:Crimson Mode
background:
The awakened form of the once-boisterous Nezhamon, assumed in order to properly utilize its own power. In this form, Nezhamon's small body has grown in size, and it now rides a motorbike, the flaming wheels of which are the transformed Fukanirin. It has also gained two pairs of adjustable cyber-arms, becoming a Warrior Digimon with six upper limbs at its disposal.
　As before its change in form, Nezhamon can make deft use of its multiple divine weapons. These weapons include the two blades, Zanyoto and Kanyoto, the rope Bakuyosaku, which Nezhamon uses to ensnare targets, its mallet Koyosho, which it uses to beat down said targets, and lastly, the Shukyu balls, which pursue and incinerate enemies. With its six arms, Nezhamon can wield every one of these divine weapons at once and with great ease, and is able to fight enemies in the name of justice from any distance, near or far.
　Nonetheless, as its belligerent nature remains intact, Nezhamon's only weakness is its tendency to resolve situations by making a ruckus, even when its cause is just.
　Nezhamon: Wrath Mode uses its Zankan Sojingeki move to slice and dice enemies in a flash, and will channel all of its power into its six divine weapons to deliver a continuous pummeling during Fushin Rokurenge, its ultimate move.

name: Shagaramon
background:
A Holy Dragon Digimon residing in Ryushogu, also known as the Dragon Crystal Palace, in the eastern area of the Digital World. Shagaramon, draped in cloth, has the ability to control the ocean and create current gates that connect various parts of the sea. It is impossible to reach the secretive Ryushogu without using these currents.
　Entrusted with the role of banishing Digimon that disturb the peace in Ryushogu, Shagaramon has been chosen by Ryugumon to be the palace's gatekeeper. Other Digimon, such as Divermon and Mermaimon, act as gatekeepers too, under the supervision of Shagaramon.
　It also has the ability to fly, and is occasionally seen soaring into the skies above the majestic palace.
　Shagaramon creates a fan-like giant wave and swings it at the enemy for its Wave Fan Blast move. With the Marine Burst, it compresses seawater into a small orb, then makes it explode. Lastly, Shagaramon will consume the enemy with a dragon made from seawater in its Azure Dragon Fang move.

name: Medusamon
background:
A Dragon Man Digimon that Digivolved from the data of a mythological monster. Whenever Medusamon engages in battle, the serpents on its head, all of which have a will of their own, drive the enemy away. As such, the main body usually assumes a graceful, defensive stance, and barely moves at all. Even if an enemy is able to endure the serpents' attacks, Medusamon's fanged boots and tail stinger will make short work of them in close-range combat.
Medusamon has a fondness for small, round Digimon. Upon finding one it likes, likely a Digimon still in its In-Training stage, Medusamon will petrify it without hesitation and take it home to add to the collection.
　For Biting Boots, Medusamon kicks opponents with its fanged boots, ripping them to shreds. In Gorgon Eye, Medusamon emits a flash from its large eye, turning everything the light touches to stone. This powerful ability rewrites the target's very texture, forcibly freezing it. The effect will remain unless Medusamon makes physical contact with the victim. For its Raging Serpentine move, Medusamon unravels its serpent braids, restoring the serpents to their original power and triggering a rampage that brings about extreme destruction regardless of the number of enemies.

name: Lamiamon
background:
A Dragon Man Digimon with delightful scales and fins that resemble a dress. Lamiamon is able to produce various types of poisons, some of which are paralytic, with others capable of dissolving solids. Lamiamon's most favored poison gradually drains the life force of its target, and it is said that this Digimon greatly enjoys seeing its enemies suffer. The umbrella it carries is used to store a supply of the poison it generates.
A Digimon extremely well-versed in poisons, its sheer expertise enables it to analyze all manner of viral strains. For this reason, Lamiamon is believed to sometimes detoxify poisoned Digimon in exchange for hefty rewards.
　For Lamiamon's Venombrella special move, it opens its umbrella, coats it with poison, and attacks while spraying poison to contaminate its surroundings. When dealing with opponents whose suffering it deems would be unworthy of attention, Lamiamon shoots a fierce stream of poison from the tip of its umbrella, causing instant death with its Toxic Splash.

name: Necromon
background:
A top-class Ghost Digimon that has absorbed data from a variety of ghosts and horrors on the Net. It wields a gravestone-shaped sword, the GreyBlade, a vicious weapon that brings death to all who touch it, despite it being completely blunt. If Necromon happens to encounter a skilled Digimon as it drifts about, it will plunge the GreyBlade into the prospective foe without making a sound. It takes great joy in absorbing the data of Digimon it defeats this way into the lantern it carries.
　The data also gets recorded in the tome it also carries, the Necronomicon, which can be summoned like a ghost in battle.
　While it has the strength to manipulate the dead, Necromon is extremely weak to light and holy power, and it will never appear before Digimon possessing such abilities.
　With its special move Soul Striker, Necromon plunges the GreyBlade into its opponent as if it were a real gravestone, piercing the Digimon's defenses and completely destroying its DigiCore. In addition, Apparition Legion summons all of the ghost data it has absorbed from Digimon into a spiritual form that annihilates the enemy like a relentless army, no matter how much it may hurt itself in the process.

name: Jougamon
background:
A Deity Digimon holding the data of a moon goddess from Chinese legend. It is a beautiful and tranquil Digimon that usually leads a solitary existence on its Kokankyu, a ship that resembles the moon. Though it does not have many chances to show it off, Jougamon is an expert of Chinese martial arts, and uses its kicking skills and high mobility to subdue its foes.
